name: Commensal bacterial indoles extended healthspan across model systems
summary: The indole study supports microbe-derived healthspan signaling but belongs mostly on the mechanism side of the baseline.
statement: Indoles from commensal bacteria extended healthspan readouts in C. elegans, Drosophila, and mice with little effect on maximal lifespan, and worm and fly effects depended on aryl hydrocarbon receptor biology.
population_or_model: C. elegans, Drosophila melanogaster, and mouse healthspan models
time_horizon: Age-related healthspan readouts across model-specific assay windows
quantitative_note: The abstract reports healthspan extension in multiple organisms and negligible effect on maximal lifespan.
caveats: This is microbe-derived metabolite evidence, not a defined live-biotherapeutic product trial.
The mouse evidence is healthspan-contextual rather than lifespan or clinical disease-prevention evidence.
The result supports engineerable mechanisms but does not by itself establish therapeutic dosing or safety.
